title: Mildly Bitter Vietnamese White Fish Noodles
description: A kid-friendly Vietnamese dish featuring white fish, dashi-extract, and a balance of bitter and gooey flavors, suitable for flexitarian diets.
instructions: Bring a large pot of water to a boil and cook the rice noodles according to the package instructions.
In a large skillet, heat the sesame oil over medium heat and add the chopped scallions, cooking until fragrant.
Add the bitter melon flesh and seeds to the skillet and cook, stirring occasionally, until they start to soften.
Add the dashi extract, fish sauce, soy sauce, and honey to the skillet, stirring to combine.
Add the white fish fillets to the skillet, spooning some of the sauce over the top of each fillet.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, until the fish is cooked through and flakes easily with a fork.
Drain the cooked noodles and add them to the skillet, tossing to combine with the sauce and fish.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve the noodles and fish hot, garnished with additional chopped scallions if desired.
